free_text: Neutron Total Cross Section of 6Li from 10 eV to 10 MeV
The time-of-flight spectra measured with the NE-110
scintillator must be corrected for 3 backgrounds,
each <1%: first, the presence of 2.23-MeV gamma rays
from neutron capture by the water moderator which
decays with a ~17.6-microsec decay time; second, the
478-keV gamma rays produced by capture in the boron
in the pyrex of the phototube which has a 1-2
microsec decay time for the 2-cm-thick NE-110
scintillator; finally, a constant room background
which is mainly due to gamma rays.
